name: Chhaya Community Development Corporation-Branch Office
description_short: Chhaya CDC is a 501(c)(3) non-profit providing free tax preparation, housing counseling, and economic justice services specifically for South Asian and Indo-Caribbean communities in New York City.
description_long:
Chhaya Community Development Corporation was founded to address the housing and economic needs of low-income South Asian and Indo-Caribbean New Yorkers. Over 20 years, the organization has served tens of thousands of individuals across New York City with a dual approach combining direct services and systemic advocacy. The organization operates two physical locations: the main office in Jackson Heights and a Richmond Hill Center, both in Queens where these communities are concentrated.

Chhaya offers a comprehensive range of free and low-cost services including free tax preparation (2026 cycle now live with appointment booking), first-time homebuyer workshops, credit-building peer lending circles, small business support and appeal assistance, tenant rights education and organizing, immigration services and advocacy, and civic engagement programs. Their Asset Building Program specifically helps individuals without established credit history join peer lending circles where members contribute monthly toward rotating loans, enabling credit score improvement. They also provide direct legal support by connecting clients with attorneys for administrative appeals and regulatory issues.

Chhaya distinguishes itself through cultural competency and language accessibility tailored to South Asian and Indo-Caribbean immigrant populations, many with limited English proficiency. The organization combines individual service delivery with community organizing and advocacy for systemic change—as demonstrated by their work holding landlords accountable and advocating for affordable housing preservation. Their peer lending circles represent an alternative to traditional credit products, building community while establishing credit. Leadership and staff appear deeply embedded in the neighborhoods they serve.

The main limitation is geographic specificity: services are designed for and concentrated in South Asian and Indo-Caribbean communities in New York City, with no indication of service availability outside these demographics or the NYC area. While free tax prep and housing counseling are genuinely free, some programs like homeownership workshops and peer lending circles may have eligibility requirements or participation expectations not fully detailed on the website. As a community-based nonprofit, service capacity may be limited compared to larger financial institutions.
pros: Free tax preparation services available with appointment booking for 2026 tax season
Culturally competent services specifically designed for South Asian and Indo-Caribbean immigrant communities
Free peer lending circles that build credit without predatory terms or interest charges
Combines direct financial services with community organizing and systemic advocacy for tenant and housing rights
First-time homebuyer workshops and asset-building programs to help renters transition to homeownership
Free small business support including legal appeal assistance connected to outside attorneys
Two physical locations in Jackson Heights and Richmond Hill with direct community access
20+ year track record serving tens of thousands with documented success stories
cons: Services limited geographically to New York City and specifically focused on South Asian/Indo-Caribbean communities
No indication of online or remote service options for those unable to visit physical locations
Website does not clearly detail eligibility requirements, wait times, or service capacity limitations
Limited information about the scope and timeline of free tax preparation (appointment availability unclear)
Peer lending circles require monthly contributions, creating barrier for those with very limited cash flow
best_for: South Asian and Indo-Caribbean immigrants in NYC seeking culturally competent financial counseling and tax preparation
First-time homebuyers in Queens (Jackson Heights, Richmond Hill) working to build credit and save for down payments
Small business owners in immigrant communities needing assistance with regulatory fines or licensing issues
Renters experiencing housing instability or landlord violations seeking tenant rights education and organizing
diagnosis: Chhaya CDC is best for South Asian and Indo-Caribbean immigrants in NYC seeking free, culturally competent financial counseling, tax preparation, or housing assistance. The main caveat is that services are geographically limited to New York City and ethnically/culturally focused, making them inaccessible to those outside these parameters or unable to visit in-person locations in Queens.
services: Asset building peer lending circles for credit building
Housing counseling and tenant rights education
Small business advising and regulatory appeal assistance
Immigration services and immigrant rights organizing
Civic engagement and voter education programs
Legal referrals and support (connected to partner attorneys)
Tenant union organizing and leadership development
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 12118 Liberty Ave, 2Nd Floor, South Richmond Hill, NY 11419
